assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions nodes will be drained and recreated but doesn't explicitly explain the atomic recreation process or contrast it with rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response doesn't specifically mention that maintenance on one slice affects all slices in the multislice environment

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response clearly states that the training job will be interrupted/terminated and that TPU nodes will be drained and recreated

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response mentions downtime can range from 30 minutes to several hours but doesn't explain the resource availability blocking mechanism

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response recommends robust checkpointing in general but doesn't specifically advise saving a checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes preparation steps with checkpoint resumption and restart scripts, addressing automatic resume capability

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response mentions requesting maintenance postponement for critical workloads, which covers the concept of deferring events

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance windows are scheduled during low-traffic periods but doesn't suggest using them to control timing for planned training breaks
